title: Quorum Queues, Raft Replication, Availability, and Data Safety · Contract Lens: Majority Loss Recovery
summary: A team declares quorum queues for ephemeral sessions, assumes any replica count survives any failure, omits confirms and manual acknowledgements, and ignores leader locality, majority loss, backlog, and upgrade behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the majority loss recovery increment in quorum queue safety and availability decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.
Measure queue leader placement, client connection locality, cross-node routing, rebalance, and single-core hot-path constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Majority Loss Recovery · Theory Majority Loss Recovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5979 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.
